LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE CITY HAS MADE A SHOWING THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO DESIGN IMMUNITY UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 830.6 SUCH THAT THE CITY CANNOT BE LIABLE FOR PLAINTIFF YI GU'S INJURIES RESULTING FROM HER CYCLING ACCIDENT. FOR FULL RULING SEE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT AND/OR .DOC (WORD) MINUTES. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR NO. 6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2019 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Cody M. Christiansen of the law offices of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers (510.646.9616) appears by telephone as counsel for petitioner/opposing party

Deputy City Attorney, Hunter W. Sims III of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-4259) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides in open court.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The City has made a showing that it is entitled to design immunity under Government Code section 830.6 such that the City cannot be liable for Plaintiff Yi Gu's injuries resulting from her cycling accident. For full ruling see reporter's transcript and/or .doc (Word) minutes.

Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR No. 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-26-2019 Continued To Aug-05-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Set For Trial On Jun-17-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
